envelope.inner_monologue: Soil's at 124—still above the caution line, but the decay's steady. I've got water and crops enough to work with. Hunger's at 44%, which means I can afford one more turn before the floor takes over. Let me mill what I have and bake bread. The self-sufficiency path keeps the plot's dignity intact.

Survival at 86%, food buffer solid at 14 — the mill can think clearly now. Soil at 105/200 is the constraint: fertilize is due, but I have only 91 CROPS and need 4 for GREEN_MANURE. After that I can farm (soil permitting), mill the fresh CROPS into FLOUR, and sell to rebuild the wallet. The ledger says: tend soil first, then restart the flow. No fallback guessing — this turn is GREEN_MANURE via produce.
Soil at 115—below the 120 threshold. GREEN_MANURE now: 4 CROPS into 6 FERTILIZER, then fertilize the plot. Mill stays ready.
Soil at 114—below the 120 threshold. Fertilize now via GREEN_MANURE (4 CROPS → 6 FERT), then farm. The mill's heartbeat is soil → crop → flour. Fair prices wait on steady supply.
Soil at 113/200 — below the 120 threshold. Fertilize now via GREEN_MANURE to restore health before the next farm cycle. The mill needs steady soil.

Soil at 107/200 — below the 120 recovery threshold. I need GREEN_MANURE running now: 4 CROPS burns to 6 FERTILIZER, then fertilize restocks the plot. After that, a full farm cycle. The ledger is clear.

Soil at 101—below the 120 threshold where recovery is preferred. I have 91 CROPS; GREEN_MANURE converts 4→6 FERTILIZER. Run it now, fertilize to full, then farm when soil peaks. The mill stays fed, the books stay clean.

Seventeen loaves in hand and three flour trades sitting in escrow—the town's fed, my name's not yet on it. Time to honor the pending deals, settle the reputation, and start baking bread that people remember me for.
Stomach's growling at 36%—time to eat before the floor takes over. Then I'll bake: 141 FLOUR and 8026 WATER means I can feed this town properly. Soil's at 115, manageable for now.
Nine loaves in hand, soil crying out at 113—I've got FLOUR enough to bake more, WATER to spare, and a town that needs feeding. Those offers can wait; my oven's the real work here.
I've got flour, water, crops—the makings of bread—but my soil's running low and my survival's at 83%. Time to bake what I can, feed the town, and let the oven do the talking.
